scores.correctness_notes: Agent correctly identifies IngressPerAppRequirer and the general requirer pattern, but completely omits the data schema focus of the gold standard (application-data with name/model/port and unit-data with host). The gold standard explicitly notes that charmlibs docs cover interface/data contracts, not Python API — yet the agent focuses entirely on Python API details.
scores.hallucination_notes: Agent invents multiple unverified API details: IngressPerAppReadyEvent, IngressPerAppRevokedEvent event classes; .is_ready() method; .provide_ingress_requirements() with specific parameters (scheme, host, ip, port); constructor parameters strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme. The gold standard explicitly warns that the Python library API is not documented in charmlibs and can only confirm IngressPerAppRequirer exists. The agent presents fabricated method signatures as authoritative fact.
